style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=top;rounded=0;fontSize=14;" value="<div><b>Biodata Registry</b><br></div><div><br></div><div>An ETL process will parse the data from Dataverse to aind-data-schema compliant metadata and store it in the Biodata registry.</div><div><br></div><div>A user can fetch, update, and instruments via the allen-metadata-service.</div><div><br></div><div>A user can upload acquisition or extracted acquisition metadata. The service will combine and transform the extracted acquisition files into a biodata-schema compliant file before adding it to the registry. <br></div><div><br></div><div><br></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="270" width="280" x="775" y="90" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> </root> </mxGraphModel> </diagram> </mxfile>

div.div:
Dataverse
Users in the lab can retrieve information about mice and procedures from this database using allen-metadata-service. An API client in Python will be made available.
The tables in Platform are not necessarily biodata-schema compliance.

path.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(213, 232, 212), rgb(31, 47, 30)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(130, 179, 102), rgb(68, 110, 44));

div.div: An ETL process will parse the data from Dataverse to aind-data-schema compliant metadata and store it in the Biodata registry.
A user can fetch, update, and instruments via the allen-metadata-service.

A user can upload acquisition or extracted acquisition metadata. The service will combine and transform the extracted acquisition files into a biodata-schema compliant file before adding it to the registry.
